title: crassula × putativa
intro: Crassula × putativa, a hybrid succulent plant, is a cross between the jade plant (Crassula ovata) and the pygmy jade plant (C. putata), known for its compact, shrub-like appearance and attractive foliage. The plant has been used in traditional medicine, particularly in Africa, where it is valued for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help to soothe wounds and prevent infection. The medicinal properties of Crassula × putativa are also believed to contribute to various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and supporting immune function. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, or tinctures, and is also incorporated into topical creams and ointments to harness its therapeutic potential.
